name: Read file
description: Reads the entire content of a text file.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file and return its content as plain text with 1-based line numbers (e.g. '10: line content'). Large files are paginated automatically — the response header shows total lines and the range shown; if there are remaining lines, a footer tells you the next offset to call. Use the `offset` parameter to read subsequent pages. Use this for source code, logs, configs, and any workspace file — NOT for agent KB files (use the read tool for those). Accepts /workspace/... absolute paths from the runpy sandbox, which are automatically mapped to the real project root. Constraints: file must be a text file (not binary) and must be 400KB or smaller.

file_path.description: Path to the file. Use /workspace/... for project files (e.g. '/workspace/app.py').

offset.description: 1-based line number to start reading from (default: 1). Use this to paginate through large files — the footer in each response tells you the next offset to use.
